calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions To Distribute Trust Assets
Off Calendar Jul-09-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for Jeffrey Connor. Blessing Ahuruonye for Carrie Green.

The Court questioned the appropriateness of the Trustee also having the roll of Attorney for the Beneficiaries and stated that it is of some concern. The Court also raised the issue of one of the Beneficiaries (Jeffrey Connor) making a statement as if both Beneficiaries have consented to the above arrangement and stated that a death certificate could be obtained from the Ontario government. Mr. Ahuruonye stated that Ms. Green was getting money in the past, that she passed away and that he was not sure if she had a right to principal. Mr. Huysentruyt stated that he has been in contact with Robert Connor, that Robert has no objection and that he can get a declaration from him. The Petition for Instructions to Distribute Trust Assets was taken under submission awaiting a signed statement by Robert Connor recognizing the potential conflict of Mr. Huysentruyt and that he waives it.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Removing Current Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-29-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor (Remainder Beneficiary). John Fallat for Western Surety Company.

The Court stated that it was appropriate to remove the Trustee, appoint a Successor Trustee and direct the Former Trustee to turn over all assets and for the assets to be placed into a blocked account. Mr. Fallat stated that the surety company does intend to pay the surcharges, and that he prepared a judgment but that Mr. Emezium did not approve it as to form. The Court granted the petition (subject to the written order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Order Removing Current Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-20-2010 Continued To Jun-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Emezium for Former Trustee. Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor. John Fallat for Western Surety Company.

Mr. Huysentruyt stated that he requested an explanation of how the funds were disbursed prior to the waiver and that he had not received them. Mr. Emezium stated that he had not received the information from his Client. The Court stated that it was inappropriate to make a surcharge order joint and several as to the bonding company, and that the order of surcharge as to the Representative must become final and then proceed to the bond issue. Mr. Fallat stated that they had no authority to pay the surcharge as they have no order with regard to liability, that he would review the probate code cited by the Court and submit a supplemental brief. Mr. Huysentruyt will review the list of disbursements and then Counsel will talk to see if they can stipulate to a waiver of any further accounting and as to the liablilty of the Trustee and the Surety Company. The matter was continued to June 22, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR#5520. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Bloom.

Petition For Order Removing Current Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Continued To Apr-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Emezium for the Former Trustee. John Fallat for Western Surety Company. Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor (Remainder Beneficiary).

Mr. Huysentruyt stated that there were not a lot of candidates to serve as Successor Trustee given the size of the Estate, that it was not possible to have a Professional Fiduciary get involved and suggested a blocked account be set up with the income being distributed only to the income Beneficiary. Mr. Fallat stated that he could prepare a judgment with regard to the Surcharge Order filed on February 19, 2010 and that a Final Account should be filed. Mr. Huysentruyt also stated that he could prepare the order removing the Trustee, appointing the Successor Trustee and setting up the blocked account. Mr. Huysentruyt will discuss a waiver of account with his Client. The Petition for Order Removing Current Trustee was continued to April 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status. The issue regarding Mr. Emezium's fees was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On The Issue Of Surcharge Of The Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor (Contigent Beneficiary). Charles Emeziem for and with Veronica Ahuruonye. John Fallat for Western Assurity Company.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. Counsel stipulated to the 10% interest not being charged if treated as principal and that the Trustee acted in good faith. Mr. Huysentruyt and Mr. Fallat also stipulated as to the offer of proof presented by Mr. Emeziem being the evidence before the Court. In addition, Counsel agreed to crediting the Trustee for the amounts presented in open court with regard to attorney's fees, trust bond payment, bank fees, and for income. The issue regarding Mr. Emeziem's previously denied fee request will be addressed on February 16, 2010. The matter will be deemed taken under submission when the bank statement of the Smith Barney account and proof of the $1,000.00 payment for reinstatement of the bond is filed.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On The Issue Of Surcharge Of The Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-17-2009 Continued To Jan-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Emezium for and with Veronica Ahuruonye. John Fallat for Western Surety Company. Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor (Remainder Beneficiary).

Mr. Fallat requested the matter regarding the amount of surcharge be set for an evidentiary hearing. The evidentiary hearing was set for January 8, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Trial briefs are due by December 31, 2009.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On The Issue Of Surcharge Of The Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-20-2009 Continued To Nov-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor (Remainder Beneficary). Charles Emeziem for and with Veronica Ahuruonye. John Fallat for Western Assurity Company.

Mr. Emeziem stated that he would stay on as Counsel for Ms. Ahuruonye. Ms. Ahuruonye stated that she did not think she had made any distributions since July of 2009. Mr. Fallat stated that it did not look like self dealings, that he would like time to conduct discovery, that Ms. Ahuruonye should be given the opportunity to take the stand and that there was no one to take over the trust. Mr. Huysentruyt stated that his Client was reluctant to take on the roll of Successor Trustee, that the principal was too small for a Professional Fiduciary to be appointed and that he would talk to his Client. The matter was continued to November 17, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An evidentiary hearing will be set on that date.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Off Calendar Aug-12-2009 Continued To Oct-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor (Remainder Beneficiary). Veronica Ahuruonye in Propria Persona.

No appearance was made by Counsel Charles Emeziem or the bonding company. Ms. Ahuruonye stated that Mr. Emeziem withdrew as her Counsel. The matter was continued to October 14, 2009 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204. Mr. Huysentruyt is to give notice to the bonding company. ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Jul-01-2009 Denied

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Account Filed By The Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-02-2009 Continued To Jun-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kelechi Emezien for and with Veronica Ahuruonye. Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor.

The Court stated that it had previously suggested a petition for Successor Conservator be filed. Mr. Huysentruyt stated that he had spoken to his client but his client was reluctant to take this on. Mr. Emeziem stated that there was authority under the trust to do distributions, that bond was filed initially for the first two years, but there is no bond now. The Court stated that distributions were made without authority. In addition, the Court asked if it suspended Ms. Ahuruonye who would serve her place. Mr. Huysentruyt stated that if Ms. Ahuruonye is out then he would recommend to his client to petition or get someone else. Mr. Emeziem stated that he had additional supporting documentation that had not been offered yet. The Court stated that it wanted Mr. Emeziem to meet with the Examiner. The Court recessed briefly. Back on the record, the Court ordered Mr. Emeziem to meet with the Examiner on June 17, 2009 at 9:30 a.m. in room 202. Mr. Emeziem was instructed to bring his additional supporting documentation with him or to have it on file ahead of time. Mr. Huysentruyt stated that he may not attend the meeting. The Court stated that Mr. Emeziem was to present proof that his client had authority to distribute and instructed him to file a declaration with the his documents/proof attached. Mr. Huysentruyt inquired whether the Court would entertain a document submitted in writing regarding this issue. The Court stated Mr. Huysentruyt could submit something in writing by June 17, 2009. The matter was continued to June 17, 2009, and is to be taken under submission as of that date.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Off Calendar Apr-21-2009 Continued To Jun-02-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor. Charles Emezium for and with Veronica Ahuruonye.

The Court stated that the submitted account was not in proper form and that further continuances would not correct the short comings in the account. The Court encouraged Mr. Huysentruyt to file a Petition For Appointment of Successor Trustee. The matter was continued to June 2, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an attempt at an amended account and for the Petition For Appointment of Successor Trustee.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Account Filed By The Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-24-2009 Continued To Apr-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor. Veronica Ahuruonye (Trustee) in Propria persona. Carrie Grene was also present.

Ms. Ahuruonye stated that her Attorney was supposed to be present. The Court explained that Counsel needed to be present to address concerns regarding the accounting. Mr. Huysentruyt expressed concern with the Attorney's fees, the accounting fees and stated that the Parties needed to demonstrate how the money was spent for the benefit of the trust. Ms. Ahuruonye stated that the expenses were due to her lack of experience and knowledge, that she paid her Attorney $550.00 to do the accounting, that she owed a balance and there was $5,000.00 to $7,000.00 left in the trust account. The Court order Ms. Ahuruonye not to pay any attorney's fees without a court order. The matter was continued to April 21, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Emeziem is ordered to appear on that date.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Off Calendar Feb-17-2009 Continued To Mar-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Veronica Ahuruonye in Propria Persona.

The Court informed Ms. Ahuruonye that the accounting needed to be filed in proper probate format, that the accounting needed to cover the period of May 19, 2004 through September 30, 2009 and suggested Ms. Ahuruonye hire an attorney with experience in trust administration to assist in preparing the accounting. Ms. Ahuruonye stated that she understood and that she was in the process of seeking professional help. The matter was continued to March 17, 2009. If the accounting is not filed, the Court will consider the removal of Ms. Ahuruonye as the Trustee.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marianne Malveaux for Veronica Ahuruonye (Respondent). Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor (Remainder Beneficiary).

Mr. Huysentruyt stated that he was still waiting. Ms. Malveaux stated that she had submitted a declaration this morning and noted that the accounting was not in the proper Probate format. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court) and set the due date for January 30, 2009.

Petition To Compel Account Of Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-20-2008 Continued To Dec-15-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for the Petitioner. Marianne Malveax for and with Veronica Ahuruonye (Respondent/Successor Trustee).

Mr. Huysentruyt stated that opposing counsel had presented a partial accounting and concurred with Ms. Malveax' request for a 45 day continuance. The parties stipulated that the last day of the account will run through September 30, 2008. Mr. Huysentruyt to file a supplement with regard to the stipulation. The matter was continued to December 15, 2008.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

Petition To Compel Account Of Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-06-2008 Continued To Oct-20-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for Dorothy Connor. Veronica Ahuruonye (Trustee) in propria persona.

Ms. Ahuruonye stated that she had hired two accountants: one to prepare the accounting and the other to assist with the trust. In addition, she had hired an attorney to file the first accounting, and she had just recently found out that the first account had not been done. Mr. Huysentruyt stated that he just recently received a call from an attorney who stated she may be representing the trustee; however, he has not heard from counsel again. Ms. Ahuruonye's request to continue the matter to October 20, 2008, was granted.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy: Aguerre.
